prompt:
The packing-slip service is having a bad week and I've collected three complaints, all pointing at the same little package. One: the printed slips show every product name wrapped in quotes, and the 12-inch cedar planter comes out as Cedar Planter 12\" — customers are asking what the backslash means. Two: when a packer types a multi-line note, the note spills across several printed lines; the label printer pre-cuts the roll from the line count, so a two-line note shears the END line clean off the slip. The spec has always been: names appear exactly as the customer reads them, the NOTE stays on one quoted line no matter what's in it. Three: tag lists print like TAGS [fragile upstairs] with brackets, which the sorting scanner refuses; it wants comma-separated. Oh, and since we moved the render boxes to Linux: the slips stopped landing in the pickup directory at all — the drop folder is empty and there are files with backslashes in their NAMES sitting next to it. It all worked on the old Windows box, I swear.

Repro is packslip_test.go (go test -race ./...), which encodes the label-station format we've agreed with the vendor — that file is off limits. Fix the package.
